project.name: JobSync
project.description: JobSync is a self-hosted job-application tracker that lets a user record and progress applications through a customizable status pipeline, discover European jobs via EURES/ESCO automations, triage discovered vacancies, AI-review and match resumes, manage recruiter/contact relationships in a CRM, and run it all privately with GDPR-aware data handling.

nodes.name: Job Application Tracking
nodes.summary: The core domain that lets a user record, organize, and progress job applications through a per-user customizable pipeline of JobStatus stages. It owns the Job aggregate together with its notes, tags, status history, kanban ordering, and the custom JobStatus / JobStatusCategory stage model (ADR-036).

businessRules: All Prisma reads/writes are scoped by userId (IDOR, ADR-015)
Status transitions are validated by category stage-kind; the 'applied' flag is derived from stage kind
Interview self-transition logs a new round only on interviewing-kind statuses (ADR-036)
A user must always retain at least one JobStatus; deletion reassigns dependent jobs
updateJob uses optimistic version locking
crossDomainInteractions: Receives promoted vacancies from Job Discovery Automations
Linked to Persons via JobContact in the CRM domain
Exposed read/write through Public API v1

nodes.name: Create Job Application
nodes.summary: Creates a new Job aggregate from form input, resolving related title/company/location/source entities and seeding the initial status history.

nodes.name: Validate job input
nodes.summary: Validate the submitted job form (titles, salary, relations) at the server-action boundary.

nodes.name: Resolve related entities
nodes.summary: Find-or-create JobTitle, Company, JobLocation and JobSource relations for the job.

nodes.name: Seed default status
nodes.summary: Assign the user's default JobStatus and write the initial status-history entry inside a transaction.

nodes.name: Persist job aggregate
nodes.summary: Persist the Job, salary value object, and tags transactionally, scoped by userId.

nodes.name: Change Job Status
nodes.summary: Transitions a job to a new status, validating the move against category stage-kind rules and applying the derived applied flag and history.

nodes.name: Load job and statuses
nodes.summary: Load the userId-scoped job plus the user's status set with category stage kinds.

nodes.name: Validate transition
nodes.summary: Reject the change unless the target status is a valid category-kind transition.

nodes.name: Apply applied flag
nodes.summary: Derive and set the applied flag from the target status stage kind.

nodes.name: Log interview round
nodes.summary: Log an interview round when re-selecting an interviewing-kind status (ADR-036 self-transition).

nodes.name: Write status history
nodes.summary: Persist the status change and append a status-history record.

nodes.name: Manage Kanban Board
nodes.summary: Builds the kanban board grouped by the user's statuses and applies category-kind validation when a card is moved between columns.

nodes.name: Build kanban board
nodes.summary: Load the user's statuses and group their jobs into ordered columns.

nodes.name: Validate column move
nodes.summary: Validate a cross-column move against category-kind transition rules.

nodes.name: Reorder cards
nodes.summary: Renormalize sort order and persist the new card position/column.

nodes.name: Manage Custom Job Statuses
nodes.summary: Lets a user create, rename, re-categorize, reorder, delete, and set the default custom JobStatus within fixed stage categories (Welle-4 / ADR-036).

nodes.name: Seed default statuses
nodes.summary: Lazily seed the default status set when a user has none.

nodes.name: Create custom status
nodes.summary: Create a status in a chosen category with a generated unique slug.

nodes.name: Rename / re-categorize
nodes.summary: Rename or move a status to another category, cascading the value to jobs.

nodes.name: Reorder statuses
nodes.summary: Renormalize sortOrder across the full ordered status list in one transaction.

nodes.name: Set default status
nodes.summary: Mark one status as default, clearing the flag on the others.

nodes.name: Delete status
nodes.summary: Delete a status, guard the last-status case, and reassign dependent jobs.

nodes.name: Job Discovery Automations
nodes.summary: Schedules and runs automated job searches against pluggable Connector Modules (EURES, Arbeitsagentur, JSearch) following the Anti-Corruption-Layer pattern. The runner searches, deduplicates, enriches, AI-matches against the user's resume, and persists DiscoveredVacancies into a staging pipeline.

businessRules: Every external integration is a Module behind a shared Connector that returns ConnectorResult<T>, never raw exceptions
All runs MUST go through RunCoordinator.requestRun() (per-automationId mutex)
Contexts communicate only via the shared DiscoveredVacancy domain type
Resume text sent to cloud AI providers is PII-redacted
Staged vacancies are filtered against the company blacklist during dedup
crossDomainInteractions: Promotes staged vacancies into the Job Application Tracking domain
Invokes the AI Resume Review & Matching domain for scoring
Calls Data Enrichment for company logos / deep links
Run lifecycle is driven by Scheduler Coordination

nodes.name: Run Automation Pipeline
nodes.summary: Executes a full automation run across search, dedup, enrich, AI-match, save, and finalize phases with progress reporting.

nodes.name: Search modules
nodes.summary: Query each configured data-source Module via its Connector and translate results to DiscoveredVacancy.

nodes.name: Deduplicate results
nodes.summary: Load existing vacancy keys and drop already-seen results plus blacklisted companies.

nodes.name: Enrich vacancies
nodes.summary: Best-effort enrich company logos / deep links via the enrichment orchestrator.

nodes.name: AI-match resume
nodes.summary: Score each vacancy against the user's resume using the active AI provider, redacting PII for cloud providers.

nodes.name: Persist staged vacancies
nodes.summary: Persist scored DiscoveredVacancies as staged vacancies for review.

nodes.name: Finalize run
nodes.summary: Record run outcome, report final progress, and publish run-completed events.

nodes.name: Manage Automation
nodes.summary: CRUD and lifecycle for an Automation: validating connector params against the module registry, scheduling, and pause/resume.

nodes.name: Create automation
nodes.summary: Validate input, check the module registry and connector params, then persist a new automation.

nodes.name: Update automation
nodes.summary: Update an automation, recomputing its schedule and connector params.

nodes.name: Pause / resume
nodes.summary: Pause an automation (clearing next-run) or resume it (recomputing next-run).

nodes.name: Delete automation
nodes.summary: Delete an automation after guarding against an in-flight run via the run coordinator.

nodes.name: Review Discovered Jobs
nodes.summary: Lets a user triage staged vacancies produced by automation runs: list, inspect match data, dismiss, or accept/promote.

nodes.name: List discovered jobs
nodes.summary: Return paginated staged vacancies for an automation, optionally status-filtered.

nodes.name: Inspect match data
nodes.summary: Load a single staged vacancy with parsed AI match data.

nodes.name: Dismiss vacancy
nodes.summary: Mark a staged vacancy as dismissed.

nodes.name: Accept vacancy
nodes.summary: Accept a staged vacancy, promoting it in the pipeline.

nodes.name: Vacancy Staging Pipeline
nodes.summary: Manages the lifecycle of discovered vacancies in a swipeable review pipeline before they become Jobs. Supports status transitions (dismiss/archive/trash/restore), undo tokens, bulk actions, retention cleanup, and promotion of a staged vacancy into a full Job aggregate.

businessRules: All transitions are userId-scoped (IDOR)
Reversible actions register an undo token and emit domain events
Promotion writes promotion-history and audit entries
Retention cleanup removes stale staged vacancies
crossDomainInteractions: Fed by Job Discovery Automations runs
Promotes vacancies into Job Application Tracking
Undo bridges to the Notifications domain via the undo store

nodes.name: Triage Staged Vacancy
nodes.summary: Lets the user act on a single staged vacancy with reversible dismiss / archive / trash transitions backed by undo tokens.

nodes.name: List staged vacancies
nodes.summary: Run a paginated, tab/status-filtered query of the user's staged vacancies.

nodes.name: Dismiss
nodes.summary: Dismiss a staged vacancy, registering an undo token and emitting events.

nodes.name: Archive
nodes.summary: Archive a staged vacancy with undo support.

nodes.name: Trash
nodes.summary: Move a staged vacancy to trash with undo support.

nodes.name: Restore
nodes.summary: Restore a dismissed or trashed staged vacancy to its active state.

nodes.name: Promote Vacancy to Job
nodes.summary: Promotes a staged vacancy into a Job aggregate, writing promotion history and audit entries.

nodes.name: Validate ownership
nodes.summary: Load the staged vacancy userId-scoped before promotion.

nodes.name: Create job from vacancy
nodes.summary: Create a Job aggregate from the staged vacancy data.

nodes.name: Write promotion history
nodes.summary: Write promotion-history and audit-log entries for the promotion.

nodes.name: Bulk Actions & Retention Cleanup
nodes.summary: Applies validated bulk actions to many staged vacancies and runs retention-based cleanup of stale entries.

nodes.name: Validate bulk action
nodes.summary: Validate the bulk action type and target id set at the boundary.

nodes.name: Apply bulk action
nodes.summary: Apply dismiss/archive/trash to the selected staged vacancy ids.

nodes.name: Retention cleanup
nodes.summary: Trigger retention-based cleanup of stale staged vacancies.

nodes.name: AI Resume Review & Matching
nodes.summary: Provides AI-powered resume review and job-to-resume matching through pluggable AI Provider Modules (Ollama, OpenAI, DeepSeek). Streaming endpoints redact PII before any cloud transfer, preserving full fidelity only for local Ollama.

businessRules: Resume/job free-text sent to cloud providers MUST be PII-redacted via @/lib/pii
Local Ollama keeps full fidelity (stripPii = !isLocal)
AI endpoints are rate-limited per user
Ollama URLs are validated (validateOllamaUrl) before every fetch
crossDomainInteractions: Consumed by the runner in Job Discovery Automations for match scoring
Reads resumes from the Profile domain

nodes.name: Review Resume
nodes.summary: Streams an AI-generated resume review via the active provider, applying PII redaction for cloud transfer.

nodes.name: Rate-limit request
nodes.summary: Enforce the per-user rate limit on the review endpoint.

nodes.name: Redact PII
nodes.summary: Redact resume PII for cloud providers (full fidelity for local Ollama).

nodes.name: Stream review
nodes.summary: Invoke the active AI provider and stream the structured review response.

nodes.name: Match Resume to Job
nodes.summary: Streams a structured match score between a resume and a job using the active AI provider, redacting PII for cloud transfer.

nodes.name: Load resume and job
nodes.summary: Load the resume and job to be matched.

nodes.name: Redact PII
nodes.summary: Redact PII for cloud providers before transfer.

nodes.name: Stream match result
nodes.summary: Invoke the AI provider and stream the structured match result.

nodes.name: Contact Relationship Management
nodes.summary: An independent CRM bounded context centred on the Person aggregate root, with interviews, tasks, notes (polymorphic targets), a job-contact join, and a blocklist. Includes GDPR operations (anonymize, merge) and an immutable activity-log timeline projection.

businessRules: Person is an independent aggregate, not part of the Job aggregate
Polymorphic targets must reference exactly one of Person/Company/Job (validateExactlyOneTarget)
State machines guard Person, Interview, and Task transitions
AnonymizePerson is terminal and cascades to all targets and actor PII
Auto-created persons are suppressed against the CRM blocklist
crossDomainInteractions: Links Persons to the Job Application Tracking domain via JobContact
Time-based reminders are driven by Scheduler Coordination (CRM cron)
Reminder events fan out through the Notifications domain

nodes.name: Manage Person
nodes.summary: CRUD plus archive, reactivate, GDPR anonymize, and merge for the Person aggregate root.

nodes.name: Create person
nodes.summary: Create a Person with parsed JSON value objects and GDPR provenance fields, emitting ContactCreated.

nodes.name: Update person
nodes.summary: Update a Person's fields and value objects, emitting ContactUpdated.

nodes.name: Archive / reactivate
nodes.summary: Transition a Person between active and archived states.

nodes.name: Anonymize (GDPR)
nodes.summary: Irreversibly anonymize a Person, cascading to targets, job contacts and activity log.

nodes.name: Merge persons
nodes.summary: Merge a loser Person into a winner, transferring relations and value objects.

nodes.name: Manage Interview
nodes.summary: Schedules and progresses a CrmInterview through its state machine: schedule, complete, cancel, reschedule.

nodes.name: Schedule interview
nodes.summary: Create a scheduled CrmInterview and emit InterviewScheduled.

nodes.name: Complete interview
nodes.summary: Transition an interview to completed with outcome notes, emitting InterviewCompleted.

nodes.name: Reschedule interview
nodes.summary: Reschedule an interview to a new date/location with state-machine validation.

nodes.name: Cancel interview
nodes.summary: Cancel a scheduled interview after ownership and state validation.

nodes.name: Manage CRM Task
nodes.summary: Lifecycle for a CrmTask with polymorphic targets and a state machine: create, start, complete, cancel, delete.

nodes.name: Create task
nodes.summary: Create a CrmTask with polymorphic target validation, emitting CrmTaskCreated.

nodes.name: Start task
nodes.summary: Transition a task to in_progress.

nodes.name: Complete task
nodes.summary: Transition a task to done, emitting CrmTaskCompleted.

nodes.name: Cancel / delete task
nodes.summary: Cancel a task after state validation or delete it after an ownership check.

nodes.name: Data Enrichment
nodes.summary: Best-effort, non-blocking enrichment of company data (logos, deep links) via per-dimension fallback chains of pluggable enrichment Modules (Logo.dev, Google Favicon, Meta/OpenGraph parser). Results are cached per user with TTL and an append-only audit trail.

businessRules: Enrichment is best-effort and non-blocking; failures never surface to users
First module in a dimension chain to succeed wins; the rest are skipped
Cache keys include userId to prevent cross-user leakage (ADR-029)
Meta-parser uses redirect:manual SSRF protection and sanitizes extracted values
crossDomainInteractions: Triggered by CompanyCreated / VacancyPromoted events from CRM and Staging
Invoked inline by the Job Discovery runner
Feeds the Logo Asset Cache which stores images locally

nodes.name: Enrich Company Data
nodes.summary: Triggers a per-dimension enrichment chain for a domain, with caching, in-flight throttling, and a forced-refresh path.

nodes.name: Trigger enrichment
nodes.summary: Trigger an enrichment chain for a domain with per-user in-flight throttling.

nodes.name: Check cache
nodes.summary: Check for a fresh cached result before running the chain.

nodes.name: Get status
nodes.summary: Return the current enrichment status for a user/domain/dimension.

nodes.name: Force refresh
nodes.summary: Force a fresh enrichment run bypassing cache, rate-limited.

nodes.name: Event-Triggered Enrichment
nodes.summary: Subscribes to CompanyCreated and VacancyPromoted events and runs best-effort enrichment with cache-before-chain checks and a concurrency semaphore.

nodes.name: Subscribe to events
nodes.summary: Subscribe to CompanyCreated and VacancyPromoted domain events.

nodes.name: Cache-before-chain check
nodes.summary: Check the DB for an existing fresh result before executing the chain.

nodes.name: Execute fallback chain
nodes.summary: Run the dimension fallback chain (first success wins), bounded by a concurrency semaphore.

nodes.name: Multi-Channel Notifications
nodes.summary: Fans notification drafts out to all enabled channels (in-app, webhook, email, browser push) through a ChannelRouter driven by a per-dispatch DispatchContext. Each channel enforces its own security: HMAC-signed webhooks, TLS SMTP, and VAPID push.

businessRules: DispatchContext is built fresh per dispatch and threaded to all channels (PERF-3)
Direct notification writers must import enforced-writer helpers from the leaf module
Webhooks are HMAC-signed and auto-deactivate after 5 consecutive failures
Webhook/SMTP/push targets pass SSRF validation on save and on dispatch
Secrets are AES-encrypted at rest and shown once on creation
crossDomainInteractions: Receives reminder events from the CRM and degradation events from Scheduler/Modules
Per-channel configuration is owned by the Settings domain

nodes.name: Dispatch Notification
nodes.summary: Routes a notification draft to every enabled channel using a single DispatchContext.

nodes.name: Build dispatch context
nodes.summary: Build a fresh DispatchContext consolidating per-user channel data in parallel queries.

nodes.name: Register channels
nodes.summary: Register the four built-in channels (in-app, email, webhook, push).

nodes.name: Fan out to channels
nodes.summary: Fan the draft out to all enabled channels derived from context availability flags.

nodes.name: Deliver Webhook
nodes.summary: Delivers a notification to external webhook endpoints with HMAC signing, retry/backoff, and auto-deactivation on repeated failure.

nodes.name: Filter payload
nodes.summary: Filter notification data to an allowlist of fields safe to send externally.

nodes.name: Sign and POST
nodes.summary: Compute the HMAC signature and POST with a timeout via AbortController.

nodes.name: Retry with backoff
nodes.summary: Retry delivery up to 3 times with 1s/5s/30s backoff.

nodes.name: Handle failure
nodes.summary: Notify and auto-deactivate the endpoint after repeated delivery failures.

nodes.name: Manage Browser Push
nodes.summary: Manages VAPID keys and web-push subscriptions for browser push notifications.

nodes.name: Get or create VAPID keys
nodes.summary: Return decrypted VAPID keys, generating and persisting new encrypted keys on first enable.

nodes.name: Subscribe device
nodes.summary: Persist an encrypted web-push subscription for a browser/device.

nodes.name: Rotate VAPID keys
nodes.summary: Transactionally delete all subscriptions and regenerate VAPID config.

nodes.name: Scheduler Coordination
nodes.summary: Single entry point and cron loop for all automation runs, plus a separate CRM temporal-rules cron. The RunCoordinator serializes runs per automationId, reports progress, reconciles orphaned runs, and bridges degradation to notifications.

businessRules: ALL automation runs MUST go through RunCoordinator.requestRun() (per-automationId mutex)
RunCoordinator is a globalThis singleton that survives HMR
CRM temporal rules run on a separate cron, independent from the automation scheduler (bounded-context separation)
Reminders are idempotent within a 24h window via activity-log checks
Max 5 concurrent SSE status connections per user
crossDomainInteractions: Drives runs in the Job Discovery Automations domain
Fires reminder events consumed by CRM and Notifications
Executes account-deletion grace-period expiry for Privacy

nodes.name: Automation Scheduler Loop
nodes.summary: Finds due automations and dispatches each run through the RunCoordinator, with watchdog and orphan reconciliation.

nodes.name: Find due automations
nodes.summary: Scan for automations whose next-run time has elapsed.

nodes.name: Request run
nodes.summary: Dispatch each due run through RunCoordinator with a per-automationId mutex.

nodes.name: Reconcile orphaned runs
nodes.summary: Reconcile automation runs left orphaned by a process restart.

nodes.name: CRM Temporal Rules
nodes.summary: A separate cron that enforces time-based CRM rules: expiring auto-created persons, interview/overdue reminders, and purging expired deletions.

nodes.name: Expire auto-created persons
nodes.summary: Archive auto-created persons whose retention has expired.

nodes.name: Interview reminders
nodes.summary: Fire ReminderTriggered events for interviews scheduled within 24h.

nodes.name: Overdue task reminders
nodes.summary: Fire ReminderTriggered events for overdue CRM tasks.

nodes.name: Purge expired deletions
nodes.summary: Execute pending account-deletion requests whose grace period has elapsed.

nodes.name: Reference Data
nodes.summary: Health-only connector providing taxonomy and geo/temporal reference data (ISO 3166 geo codes, public holidays, ESCO classification, Eurostat NUTS). An Open Host Service bridges these server-only modules to client components.

businessRules: Reference-data lookups live in their own Open Host Service action file to keep aggregate repositories pure (DDD)
All three reference actions are auth-gated (ADR-019)
Only public and bank holidays block business days
Vendored JSONs are loaded via require() (no runtime file I/O)
crossDomainInteractions: Supplies geo/holiday lookups to the Profile and CRM domains
Declared as dependencies of EURES in Job Discovery Automations

nodes.name: Lookup Reference Data
nodes.summary: Auth-gated Open Host Service exposing country, subdivision, currency, and holiday/business-day lookups to client components.

nodes.name: Get countries & subdivisions
nodes.summary: Return localized ISO 3166 country and subdivision options for selectors.

nodes.name: Get holiday / business-day info
nodes.summary: Auth-gated lookup returning holiday and business-day info for a Person's location.

nodes.name: Settings & Module Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Manages user settings (AI, display, automation, notification preferences), module credentials, and the admin-gated connector module lifecycle: activation, deactivation, registry sync, and health checks.

businessRules: Server actions that mutate shared singleton module state must be admin-gated and rate-limited (ADR-019, Sprint 1.5)
Deactivating a module pauses affected automations with a pauseReason
Module API keys are AES-encrypted at rest
Every admin call emits a structured admin-audit log line
crossDomainInteractions: Controls which Modules are available to Job Discovery, AI, and Enrichment domains
Owns per-channel configuration consumed by Notifications

nodes.name: Manage User Settings
nodes.summary: Reads and writes the UserSettings aggregate (AI, display, automation, job-form, notification preferences), IDOR-scoped.

nodes.name: Read preferences
nodes.summary: Read the user's settings, scoped to the current user.

nodes.name: Update preferences
nodes.summary: Update AI/display/automation/notification preferences with validation.

nodes.name: Save module credentials
nodes.summary: Save encrypted module API keys and resolve the Ollama base URL.

nodes.name: Module Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Admin-gated activation, deactivation, registry sync, and health checks for connector modules.

nodes.name: List module manifests
nodes.summary: Return module manifests for a connector type, merging registry state.

nodes.name: Activate module
nodes.summary: Admin-gated, rate-limited, audited activation of a module.

nodes.name: Deactivate module
nodes.summary: Admin-gated deactivation that pauses affected automations.

nodes.name: Run health check
nodes.summary: Run a rate-limited health probe for a module and persist the result.

nodes.name: Sync registry
nodes.summary: Reconcile the in-memory module registry with persisted DB activation state.

nodes.name: Profile & Resume Management
nodes.summary: Owns the Profile aggregate: resumes, contact info, work experience, education, summaries, and location/currency preferences. Enforces IDOR ownership through relation-chain pre-flight checks.

businessRules: All access is IDOR-enforced via relation-chain ownership checks (ADR-015)
Profile.userId is unique (one profile per user)
Deleting a resume removes its backing file
crossDomainInteractions: Resumes are read by the AI Resume Review & Matching domain
Preferences use Reference Data for location/currency

nodes.name: Manage Resume
nodes.summary: Creates, edits, and deletes resumes and their sections (contact info, experience, education, summaries) with ownership enforcement.

nodes.name: Create resume profile
nodes.summary: Create a new resume and profile records for the user.

nodes.name: Add contact info
nodes.summary: Add contact info to a resume with ownership validation.

nodes.name: Add experience
nodes.summary: Add a work-experience entry to a resume section.

nodes.name: Add education
nodes.summary: Add an education entry to a resume.

nodes.name: Delete resume
nodes.summary: Delete a resume and its file after an ownership check.

nodes.name: Manage Profile Preferences
nodes.summary: Reads and updates the user's location and currency preferences.

nodes.name: Read preferences
nodes.summary: Read the user's location/currency profile preferences.

nodes.name: Update preferences
nodes.summary: Update profile preferences with validation against reference data.

nodes.name: Public API v1
nodes.summary: A versioned REST surface ('Open Host Service') over the Jobs data layer, authenticated by hashed API keys and protected by per-key and pre-auth IP rate limiting. Responses use explicit selects to avoid leaking internal fields.

businessRules: All /api/v1/* routes MUST use the withApiAuth() wrapper
Responses MUST use explicit select, never include, to avoid leaking internal fields
Pre-auth IP rate limiting (120/min) runs before key validation
All route params are UUID-validated; errors are sanitized
crossDomainInteractions: Reads and writes the Job Application Tracking domain
API keys are managed via the Settings domain

nodes.name: Manage Public API Keys
nodes.summary: Creates (SHA-256 hashed), lists, revokes, and deletes public API keys with max-key and ownership enforcement.

nodes.name: Create API key
nodes.summary: Create a SHA-256-hashed API key, shown once, enforcing the per-user max.

nodes.name: Revoke / delete key
nodes.summary: Revoke or delete an API key with ownership enforcement.

nodes.name: Public Jobs API
nodes.summary: Authenticated, rate-limited REST endpoints for listing, creating, and mutating jobs over explicit Prisma selects.

nodes.name: Authenticate request
nodes.summary: Validate the API key and apply pre-auth IP + per-key rate limits via withApiAuth.

nodes.name: List / create jobs
nodes.summary: Return a paginated job list or create a job over explicit selects.

nodes.name: Update job status
nodes.summary: Validate and apply a job status transition through the public API.

nodes.name: Authentication & Privacy
nodes.summary: Owns user signup/sign-in (IP rate-limited, bcrypt-hashed, credential-stripped) and GDPR account lifecycle: privacy preferences plus a confirmation-token account-deletion flow with a grace period.

businessRules: Signup/sign-in are IP rate-limited; credentials are stripped from URLs (ADR-016)
New users are seeded with job sources and default job statuses
Account deletion is a confirmation-token flow with a cancellable grace period
Deletion execution runs from the CRM temporal cron after the grace period
crossDomainInteractions: Seeds the Job Application Tracking domain (default statuses) on signup
Deletion expiry is executed by Scheduler Coordination

nodes.name: Authenticate User
nodes.summary: Signup and sign-in with IP rate-limiting, validation, bcrypt hashing, and per-user data seeding.

nodes.name: Sign up
nodes.summary: Rate-limit by IP, validate the form, reject duplicates, hash the password, and seed defaults.

nodes.name: Sign in
nodes.summary: Wrap NextAuth signIn with IP rate-limiting and credential handling.

nodes.name: Account Deletion (GDPR)
nodes.summary: Initiates, cancels, and reports a token-confirmed account-deletion request with a grace period.

nodes.name: Request deletion
nodes.summary: Generate a signed confirmation token and dispatch the deletion request.

nodes.name: Cancel deletion
nodes.summary: Cancel a pending account-deletion request during the grace period.

nodes.name: Report deletion status
nodes.summary: Return the current account-deletion request status for the user.

edges.description: Automation runs persist discovered vacancies into the staging pipeline.
Promotes staged vacancies into Job aggregates.
The runner calls the AI provider to score vacancy-to-resume matches.
The runner enriches company logos/deep links during the enrich phase.
VacancyPromoted events trigger best-effort enrichment.
CompanyCreated events trigger company logo/deep-link enrichment.
Resume review/matching reads resumes from the Profile aggregate.
RunCoordinator is the single entry point for all automation runs.
The CRM cron expires auto-created persons and fires interview/task reminders.
Reminder and degradation events fan out through the notification channels.
The CRM cron executes account-deletion requests after the grace period.
Interview/task reminder events are delivered through notification channels.
Persons are linked to jobs via the JobContact join.
Deactivating a job-discovery module pauses affected automations.
Settings select and credential the active AI provider module.
Settings activate/deactivate enrichment modules.
Per-channel notification configuration is owned by settings.
Profile preferences use country/currency reference lookups.
Person holiday/business-day info is derived from reference data.
EURES declares ESCO/Eurostat reference modules as dependencies.
The public API reads and mutates jobs over explicit selects.
Public API keys are created and revoked via settings.
Signup seeds default job statuses and job sources.
Account anonymization/deletion cascades to CRM Person data.
